topic.title: Subject: Announcing the NortheasternISE Substack: Call for Contributors!
topic.text:
Subject: Announcing the NortheasternISE Substack: Call for Contributors!

 

Hello everyone,

We are excited to announce the launch of our new department hub: the NortheasternISE Substack (https://substack.com/@NortheasternISE ). This publication is the official voice for our unit as we transition to Information and Software Engineering, and we are looking for contributions in all of the following sections:

 

Reviews: Critiques of research papers on AI ethics and computational skepticism, as well as reviews of books ranging from technical foundations to classic literature.

 

AI Skunkworks: Showcasing technical breakthroughs and innovative projects from our students and alumni, including Humanitarians AI initiatives.

 

Insights: Academic content featuring Lab Spotlights (like the Medhavy tool), Curriculum Previews for new courses, and official Department Updates.

 

Dana Hub: The community heartbeat for logistics regarding Co-ops, Hackathons, the ISE Speaker Series, and networking events.

 

Husky Living: A student-led guide to life at Northeastern, covering housing tips for Boston and Nashville, dining reviews, and campus culture.

 

Co-ops & Careers: Strategic roadmaps for the job market, focusing on portfolio-driven success and "Proof of Work" spotlights.

 

How to Contribute:

Request Access: You must use your official NEU email address to contact the editor at mgen-substack@northeastern.edu.

 

Get Onboarded: Once verified, you will be added as a Contributor on Substack and granted access to the Claude writing tool to assist with your drafts.

Multimedia Requirement: All submissions must include high-quality images and video to complement the text.

 

Editor Review: When your draft is complete, set the publication date for exactly one week in the future. This allows the editorial team time to review, refine, or approve your contribution before it goes live.

 

We look forward to showcasing your work and building this community together.

 

Best regards,

Nik Bear Brown, PhD Associate Teaching Professor College of Engineering, Northeastern University
